conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me first review the official eligibility rules, and then I'll ask you for the relevant information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, can you tell me: **Do you have any children you're looking after, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's living at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'd like to ask you a few questions about Alex to assess your eligibility.

**How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Alex is 20 years old, I need to ask:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the official rules I've reviewed, I now have enough information to make a determination about your eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**I'm sorry, but you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why: Child Benefit is normally paid for children under 16 years old. For children aged 16 or over, you can still qualify for Child Benefit if they are under 20 **and** they stay in approved education or training.

Since Alex is 20 years old and is not currently in approved education or training, he falls outside the age criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.

If you have any other questions or if Alex's circumstances change (for example, if he were to start approved education or training), please feel free to ask.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
circumstances: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 20 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit is normally paid for children under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you can still qualify for Child Benefit if they are under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 years old and not in approved education or training, he falls outside the age criteria for Child Benefit eligibility. Therefore, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 20 years old and not in approved education or training. Under UK Child Benefit rules, children aged 20 or over do not qualify for Child Benefit unless they were already receiving it while under 20 and in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 and not in education or training, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

For children aged 16 or over, you can still qualify for Child Benefit if they are under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 years old and not in approved education or training, he falls outside the age criteria for Child Benefit eligibility. Therefore, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 20 years old and not in approved education or training. Under UK Child Benefit rules, children aged 20 or over do not qualify for Child Benefit unless they were already receiving it while under 20 and in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 and not in education or training, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
